title: doppler: zeroization gap in get() is fixable using extract_path_field but comment incorrectly says no tool exists
description: doppler/src/lib.rs comment says: 'Fixing this gap requires either a Zeroizing-aware JSON library or a custom nested extractor. Neither exists in this codebase today.' This is wrong. SecretValue::extract_path_field() exists in secretx-core and handles exactly this case: navigate nested objects (['secret']) then extract a named field ('computed'). Fix: replace resp.json().await (which leaks all field values into plain String allocations via serde_json) with: let body = resp.bytes().await?; let sv = SecretValue::new(body.to_vec()); sv.extract_path_field(&["secret"], "computed"). The response body is then in a Zeroizing buffer; only the key strings in navigation are non-Zeroizing, not the secret value.
